Quote: The frost performs its secret ministry.
Author: Samuel Taylor Coleridge

Quote: If Winter comes, can Spring be far behind?
Author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

Quote: The winds of March were wild and chill.
Author: Bayard Taylor

Quote: April hath put a spirit of youth in everything.
Author: William Shakespeare

Quote: A little Madness in the Spring is wholesome.
Author: Emily Dickinson

Quote: And what is so rare as a day in June?
Author: James Russell Lowell

Quote: The livelong day with open windowed eye.
Author: Walt Whitman

Quote: Season of mists and mellow fruitfulness.
Author: John Keats

Quote: The morns are meeker than they were.
Author: Emily Dickinson

Quote: The mellow year is hasting to its close.
Author: William Cullen Bryant

Quote: No warmth, no cheerfulness, no healthful ease,
Author: Thomas Hood

Quote: In the bleak midwinter, frosty wind made moan.
Author: Christina Rossetti
